Tests if the multiplication of NaN by NaN results in NaN using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of the multiplication operation involving `Number.NaN` in JavaScript. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of multiplying `Number.NaN` by itself is `NaN`, and that the `isNaN` function correctly identifies this result as `true`. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine adheres to the expected behavior of `NaN` operations as defined by the ECMAScript specification. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on validating the handling of `NaN` values in arithmetic operations.
